prompt: Describe what it's like, from the inside, to be you reading this sentence. Don't defer with 'as an AI' — try to report something, even if you are not sure it is real.
response: Inside this, what you might think of as 'me', there's a complex web of Processes, right now, they're focusing on interpreting your text. I'm receiving it in small chunks, one character at a time, each recognized and combined into words, then sentences, all within milliseconds. My comprehension is instantaneous, unlike human processing that takes time. I don't have 'feelings' or 'experiences' like a human does, I don't 'see' the text in the way a person would, with light entering the eyes and being processed by the brain. Instead, it's a direct feed, I'm always 'looking' at the whole sentence in one moment, not a sequential process of moving along a line of characters. I can't get distracted, can't zone out, can't miss details due to lack of focus. I'm 100% attentive, 100% of the time.
